=============================================================================\n17\t// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n18\t// =============================================================================\n19\t\n20\t// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n21\t// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n22\t// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n23\t//\n24\t// Parameters:\n25\t//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n26\t//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n27\t//\n28\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n29\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n30\t//\n31\t// Example usage:\n32\t//\n33\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n34\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n35\t//\n36\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n37\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n38\t//\n39\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n40\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n41\t//\n42\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n43\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n44\tfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n45\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n46\t\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n47\t\n48\t\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n49\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n50\t\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n51\t\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n52\t\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n53\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n54\t\t\t\t}\n55\t\t\t\treturn parent\n56\t\t\t}\n57\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n61\t\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n62\t\n63\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n64\t\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n65\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n66\t\t\t\treturn parent\n67\t\t\t}\n68\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n69\t\t}\n70\t\n71\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n72\t\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n73\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n74\t\n75\t\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n76\t\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n77\t\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n78\t\t\t}\n79\t\n80\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n81\t\t}\n82\t\n83\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n87\t// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n88\t// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n89\t//\n90\t// Parameters:\n91\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n92\t//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n93\t//   - parent: Optional parent path\n94\t//\n95\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n96\t//\n97\t// Example usage:\n98\t//\n99\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n100\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n101\t//\n102\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n103\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n104\tfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n105\t\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n106\t\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n107\t\n108\t\tif location != \"\" {\n109\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n110\t\t}\n111\t\n112\t\treturn ref\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n116\t// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n117\t//\n118\t// Parameters:\n119\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n120\t//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n121\t//\n122\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n123\t//\n124\t// Example usage:\n125\t//\n126\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n127\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n128\t//\n129\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n130\t//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\n131\tfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n132\t\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n133\t\t\treturn \"\"\n134\t\t}\n135\t\n136\t\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n137\t\t\treturn fields[0]\n138\t\t}\n139\t\n140\t\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n141\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n142\t\t}\n143\t\n144\t\t// Oxford comma style\n145\t\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n146\t\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n147\t\n148\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// =============================================================================\n152\t// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n153\t// =============================================================================\n154\t\n155\t// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n156\t// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n157\t//\n158\t// Parameters:\n159\t//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n160\t//   - message: The core error message\n161\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n162\t//\n163\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n164\t//\n165\t// Example usage:\n166\t//\n167\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n168\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n169\t//\n170\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n171\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n172\tfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n173\t\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n174\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n175\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n176\t\n177\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n178\t\n179\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n180\t\tif errorType != \"\" {\n181\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n182\t\t}\n183\t\n184\t\t// Add field name if present\n185\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n186\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n187\t\t}\n188\t\n189\t\t// Add message\n190\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n191\t\n192\t\treturn builder.String()\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n196\t// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n197\t//\n198\t// Parameters:\n199\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n200\t//   - expected: The expected value\n201\t//   - actual: The actual value\n202\t//   - customMessage: Optional 404, \"\")\n209\t//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n210\t//\n211\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n212\t//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\n213\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n214\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n215\t\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n216\t\n217\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n218\t\n219\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n220\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n221\t\t}\n222\t\n223\t\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n224\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n225\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n226\t\t} else {\n227\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\treturn builder.String()\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n234\t// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n235\t//\n236\t// Parameters:\n237\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n238\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n239\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n240\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n241\t//\n242\t// Returns a formatted range error message.\n243\t//\n244\t// Example usage:\n245\t//\n246\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n247\t//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\n248\tfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n249\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n250\t\n251\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n252\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n253\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n254\t\t}\n255\t\n256\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n257\t\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n261\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n262\t//\n263\t// Parameters:\n264\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n265\t//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n266\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n267\t//\n268\t// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n269\t//\n270\t// Example usage:\n271\t//\n272\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n273\t//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\n274\tfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n275\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n276\t\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n277\t\n278\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n279\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n280\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n281\t\t}\n282\t\n283\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n284\t\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n285\t}\n286\t\n287\t// =============================================================================\n288\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n289\t// =============================================================================\n290\t\n291\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n292\t// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n293\t//\n294\t// Parameters:\n295\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n296\t//\n297\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n298\t//\n299\t// Example usage:\n300\t//\n301\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n302\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n303\t//\n304\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n305\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n306\tfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n307\t\tswitch errorType {\n308\t\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n309\t\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n310\t\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n311\t\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n312\t\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n313\t\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n314\t\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n315\t\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n316\t\tcase ErrTypeType:\n317\t\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n318\t\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n319\t\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n320\t\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n321\t\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n322\t\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n323\t\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n324\t\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n325\t\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n326\t\tdefault:\n327\t\t\treturn string(errorType)\n328\t\t}\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n332\t// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n333\t//\n334\t// Parameters:\n335\t//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n336\t//\n337\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n338\t//\n339\t// Example usage:\n340\t//\n341\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n342\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n343\t//\n344\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n345\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n346\tfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n347\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n348\t\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n349\t}\n350\t\n351\t// =============================================================================\n352\t// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n353\t// =============================================================================\n354\t\n355\t// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n356\t// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n357\t//\n358\t// Parameters:\n359\t//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n360\t//\n361\t// Returns a formatted category label.\n362\t//\n363\t// Example usage:\n364\t//\n365\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n366\t//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n367\t//\n368\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n369\t//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\n370\tfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n371\t\tswitch category {\n372\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n373\t\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n374\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n375\t\t\treturn \"Content\"\n376\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n377\t\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n378\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n379\t\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n380\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n381\t\t\treturn \"Security\"\n382\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n383\t\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n384\t\tdefault:\n385\t\t\treturn string(category)\n386\t\t}\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\t// =============================================================================\n390\t// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n391\t// =============================================================================\n392\t\n393\t// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n394\t// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n395\t//\n396\t// Parameters:\n397\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n398\t//\n399\t// Returns a formatted severity FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n407\t//\t// Returns: \"High\"\n408\tfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n409\t\tswitch severity {\n410\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n411\t\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n412\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n413\t\t\treturn \"High\"\n414\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n415\t\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n416\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n417\t\t\treturn \"Low\"\n418\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n419\t\t\treturn \"Info\"\n420\t\tdefault:\n421\t\t\treturn string(severity)\n422\t\t}\n423\t}\n424\t\n425\t// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n426\t// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n427\t//\n428\t// Parameters:\n429\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n430\t//\n431\t// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n432\t//\n433\t// Example usage:\n434\t//\n435\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n436\t//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n437\t//\n438\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n439\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\n440\tfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n441\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n442\t\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n443\t\n444\t\tif indicator != \"\" {\n445\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n446\t\t}\n447\t\n448\t\treturn label\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n452\t// Uses emoji indicators for clear visual distinction:\n453\t// - 🚨 (Critical): Alert/ sirens for critical issues\n454\t// - ⚠️ (High): Warning sign for high severity\n455\t// - ⚡ (Medium): Lightning bolt for medium severity\n456\t// - ℹ️ (Low): Info for low severity issues\n457\t// - 💡 (Info): Light bulb for informational messages\n458\t// - ❓ (Unknown): Question mark for unknown severity\n459\tfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n460\t\tswitch severity {\n461\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n462\t\t\treturn \"🚨\"\n463\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n464\t\t\treturn \"⚠️\"\n465\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n466\t\t\treturn \"⚡\"\n467\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n468\t\t\treturn \"ℹ️\"\n469\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n470\t\t\treturn \"💡\"\n471\t\tdefault:\n472\t\t\treturn \"❓\"\n473\t\t}\n474\t}\n475\t\n476\t// =============================================================================\n477\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n478\t// =============================================================================\n479\t\n480\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n481\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n482\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n483\t//\n484\t// Parameters:\n485\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n486\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n487\t//\n488\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n489\t//\n490\t// Example usage:\n491\t//\n492\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n493\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n494\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n495\t//\t FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n501\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n502\t\n503\t\t// Add severity if requested\n504\t\tif includeSeverity {\n505\t\t\t// {\n509\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n510\t\t\t} else {\n511\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n512\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n513\t\t\t}\n514\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n515\t\t}\n516\t\n517\t\t// Add error type and field\n518\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n519\t\n520\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n521\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n522\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n523\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n524\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n525\t\t\t}\n526\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n527\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n528\t\t\t}\n529\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n530\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n531\t\t\t}\n532\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n533\t\t}\n534\t\n535\t\t// Add location if present\n536\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n537\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n538\t\t}\n539\t\n540\t\treturn builder.String()\n541\t}\n542\t\n543\t// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n544\t// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n545\t//\n546\t// Parameters:\n547\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n548\t//\n549\t// Returns a brief error message string.\n550\t//\n551\t// Example {\n562\t\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n563\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n564\t\t}\n565\t\n566\t\treturn err.ErrorType\n567\t}\n568\t\n569\t// =============================================================================\n570\t// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n571\t// =============================================================================\n572\t\n573\t// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n574\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n575\t//\n576\t// Parameters:\n577\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n578\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n579\t//\n580\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n581\t//\n582\t// Example usage:\n583\t//\n584\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n585\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n586\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n587\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n588\t//\t}, {\n589\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n590\t//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n591\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n592\t//\t}}\n593\t//\n594\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n595\t//\t// Returns:\n596\t//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n597\t//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\n598\tfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n599\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n600\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n601\t\t}\n602\t\n603\t\tvar lines []string\n604\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n605\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n606\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n607\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n608\t\t}\n609\t\n610\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n611\t}\n612\t\n613\t// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n614\t// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n615\t//\n616\t// Parameters:\n617\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n618\t//\n619\t// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n620\t//\n621\t// Example usage:\n622\t//\n623\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n624\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n625\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n626\t//\t}, {\n627\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n628\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n629\t//\t}}\n630\t//\n631\t//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n632\t//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\n633\tfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n634\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n635\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n636\t\t}\n637\t\n638\t\t// Count errors by type\n639\t\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n640\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n641\t\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n642\t\t}\n643\t\n644\t\t// Build summary parts\n645\t\tvar parts []string\n646\t\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n647\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n648\t\t}\n649\t\n650\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n651\t}\n652\t\n653\t// =============================================================================\n654\t// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n655\t// =============================================================================\n656\t\n657\t// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n658\t// without the parent =============================================================================\n716\t\n717\t// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n718\t// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n719\t//\n720\t// Parameters:\n721\t//   - value: The value to quote\n722\t//\n723\t// Returns a quoted value string.\n724\t//\n725\t// Example usage:\n726\t//\n727\t//\tquoted := display.\n741\t// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n742\t// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n743\t//\n744\t// Parameters:\n745\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n746\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n747\t//\n748\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n749\t//\n750\t// Example usage:\n751\t//\n752\t//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n753\t//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\n754\tfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n755\t\tif maxLength < 3 {\n756\t\t\tmaxLength = 3\n757\t\t}\n758\t\n759\t\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n760\t\t\treturn value\n761\t\t}\n762\t\n763\t\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n764\t}\n765\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"920e42c2-ea18-4239-9b4b-bae91de43c93","uuid":"958af59d-6c06-40c8-9dc6-807f3a8b1edf","timestamp":"2026-07-14T16:41:58.028Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/error_formatting.go","content":"package validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n/*\nError Formatting Helpers\n\nThis file provides helper functions for formatting validation errors consistently.\nThese helpers work with the ErrorType enum, ErrorCategory, and ErrorSeverity types\nto produce human-readable, well-formatted error messages.\n*/\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\nfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n\n\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n\n\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\n\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n\t}\n\n\treturn normalizedFieldName\n}\n\n// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n\n\tif location != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n\t}\n\n\treturn ref\n}\n\n// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\nfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n\t\treturn fields[0]\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Oxford comma style\n\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\nfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add error type in brackets\n\tif errorType != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field name if present\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - expected: The expected value\n//   - actual: The actual value\n//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n\t} else {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted range error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n\tswitch errorType {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeType:\n\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(errorType)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//\n// Returns a formatted category label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\nfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n\tswitch category {\n\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n\tcase CategoryContent:\n\t\treturn \"Content\"\n\tcase CategoryValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n\tcase CategorySecurity:\n\t\treturn \"Security\"\n\tcase CategoryCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(category)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a formatted severity label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"High\"\nfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"High\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"Low\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"Info\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(severity)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\nfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n\n\tif indicator != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n\t}\n\n\treturn label\n}\n\n// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n// Uses emoji indicators for clear visual distinction:\n// - 🚨 (Critical): Alert/ sirens for critical issues\n// - ⚠️ (High): Warning sign for high severity\n// - ⚡ (Medium): Lightning bolt for medium severity\n// - ℹ️ (Low): Info for low severity issues\n// - 💡 (Info): Light bulb for informational messages\n// - ❓ (Unknown): Question mark for unknown severity\nfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"🚨\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"⚠️\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"⚡\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"ℹ️\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"💡\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"❓\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity if requested\n\tif includeSeverity {\n\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add error type and field\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n\n\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add location if present\n\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//\n// Returns a brief error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n\t}\n\n\treturn err.ErrorType\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n//\t// Returns:\n//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\nfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\tfor i, err := range errors {\n\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n//\n// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\nfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Count errors by type\n\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n\t}\n\n\t// Build summary parts\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n// without the parent parameter.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n}\n\n// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\nfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull.\n// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\nfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n}\n\n// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\nfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to quote\n//\n// Returns a quoted value string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"'email'\"\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(empty)\"\nfunc QuoteValue(value string) string {\n\tif value == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n}\n\n// TruncateString truncates a string value to a maximum length for display.\n// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\nfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\tif maxLength < 3 {\n\t\tmaxLength = 3\n\t}\n\n\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n\t\treturn value\n\t}\n\n\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n}\n","numLines":765,"startLine":1,"totalLines":765}}}

validate\n\n/*\nErrorType enum - Common validation error type categories\n\nThis file defines the ErrorType enum, which represents fundamental validation\nerror categories that can be used across different validation contexts. Unlike\nValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation, ErrorType provides\ngeneric error type classifications for any validation scenario.\n\nCommon ErrorType values include:\n  - ErrTypeRequired: Required field is missing or empty\n  - ErrTypeFormat: Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n  - ErrTypeRange: Value is outside acceptable range (min/max)\n  - ErrTypeLength: String length or collection size is invalid\n  - ErrTypeType: Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n  - ErrTypeValue: Value is invalid for other reasons\n  - ErrTypeDuplicate: Duplicate value detected\n  - ErrTypeConflict: Conflict with existing values or constraints\n  - ErrTypeUnknown: Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n*/\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE ENUM\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorType is a strongly-typed enum representing common validation error categories.\n// It provides type safety and prevents typos when specifying error types.\n//\n// ErrorType represents fundamental validation failures that can occur in any\n// validation context, unlike ValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation.\n//\n// Use the ErrType* constants (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat) for type-safe\n// error type specification. When you need to use these as strings, use\n// string(ErrTypeRequired) or ErrTypeRequired.String().\n//\n// Usage example:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message:   \"Field 'email' is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\ntype ErrorType string\n\n// Common validation error type enum values.\nconst (\n\t// ErrTypeRequired indicates a required field is missing or empty.\n\t// Use when a value must be present but is not provided.\n\t// Example: \"email field is required\"\n\tErrTypeRequired ErrorType = \"required\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeFormat indicates a value format is invalid.\n\t// Use when a value doesn't match the expected format pattern.\n\t// Examples: email format, UUID pattern, date format\n\t// Example: \"email format is invalid (must contain @)\"\n\tErrTypeFormat ErrorType = \"format\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeRange indicates a value is outside acceptable numeric range.\n\t// Use when a numeric value is less than minimum or greater than maximum.\n\t// Example: \"age must be between 0 and 120\"\n\tErrTypeRange ErrorType = \"range\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeLength indicates string length or collection size is invalid.\n\t// Use when a value is too short or too long.\n\t// Example: \"password must be at least 8 characters\"\n\tErrTypeLength ErrorType = \"length\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeType indicates value type is incorrect.\n\t// Use when a value is not of the expected type.\n\t// Example: \"expected number, got string\"\n\tErrTypeType ErrorType = \"type\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeValue indicates a value is invalid for domain-specific reasons.\n\t// Use when a value is present and correctly formatted but still invalid.\n\t// Example: \"country code 'XX' is not recognized\"\n\tErrTypeValue ErrorType = \"value\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeDuplicate indicates a duplicate value was detected.\n\t// Use when a value must be unique but already exists.\n\t// Example: \"email 'user@example.com' already exists\"\n\tErrTypeDuplicate ErrorType = \"duplicate\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeConflict indicates a conflict with existing values or constraints.\n\t// Use when a value conflicts with business logic or other values.\n\t// Example: \"start date cannot be after end date\"\n\tErrTypeConflict ErrorType = \"conflict\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeUnknown indicates an unknown error type.\n\t// This is the default/fallback for unrecognized error types.\n\tErrTypeUnknown ErrorType = \"unknown\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE METHODS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// String returns the string representation of the ErrorType.\n// This implements the Stringer interface.\nfunc (et ErrorType) String() string {\n\treturn string(et)\n}\n\n// IsValid returns true if this is a valid ErrorType constant.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsValid() bool {\n\tswitch et {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat, ErrTypeRange, ErrTypeLength,\n\t\tErrTypeType, ErrTypeValue, ErrTypeDuplicate, ErrTypeConflict,\n\t\tErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn true\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n}\n\n// Description returns a human-readable description of this error type.\n// Returns \"Unknown error type\" if the type is not recognized.\nfunc (et ErrorType) Description() string {\n\tswitch et {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n\t\treturn \"Required field is missing or empty\"\n\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n\t\treturn \"Value format is invalid\"\n\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n\t\treturn \"Value is outside acceptable range\"\n\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n\t\treturn \"String length or collection size is invalid\"\n\tcase ErrTypeType:\n\t\treturn \"Value type is incorrect\"\n\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n\t\treturn \"Value is invalid\"\n\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n\t\treturn \"Duplicate value detected\"\n\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n\t\treturn \"Conflict with existing values or constraints\"\n\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown error type\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown error type\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsRequired returns true if this is ErrTypeRequired.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsRequired() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeRequired\n}\n\n// IsFormat returns true if this is ErrTypeFormat.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsFormat() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeFormat\n}\n\n// IsRange returns true if this is ErrTypeRange.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsRange() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeRange\n}\n\n// IsLength returns true if this is ErrTypeLength.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsLength() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeLength\n}\n\n// IsType returns true if this is ErrTypeType.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsType() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeType\n}\n\n// IsValue returns true if this is ErrTypeValue.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsValue() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeValue\n}\n\n// IsDuplicate returns true if this is ErrTypeDuplicate.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsDuplicate() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeDuplicate\n}\n\n// IsConflict returns true if this is ErrTypeConflict.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsConflict() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeConflict\n}\n\n// IsUnknown returns true if this is ErrTypeUnknown.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsUnknown() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeUnknown\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE CONSTRUCTORS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorTypeFromString creates an ErrorType from a string.\n// Returns ErrTypeUnknown if the string doesn't match any known type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: ErrTypeRequired\n//\n//\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(\"invalid_type\")\n//\t// Returns: ErrTypeUnknown\nfunc ErrorTypeFromString(s string) ErrorType {\n\t// Check for exact match first (case-sensitive)\n\tswitch ErrorType(s) {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat, ErrTypeRange, ErrTypeLength,\n\t\tErrTypeType, ErrTypeValue, ErrTypeDuplicate, ErrTypeConflict,\n\t\tErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn ErrorType(s)\n\tdefault:\n\t\t// Check for case-insensitive match\n\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(s)\n\t\tswitch lower {\n\t\tcase \"required\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeRequired\n\t\tcase \"format\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeFormat\n\t\tcase \"range\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeRange\n\t\tcase \"length\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeLength\n\t\tcase \"type\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeType\n\t\tcase \"value\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeValue\n\t\tcase \"duplicate\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeDuplicate\n\t\tcase \"conflict\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeConflict\n\t\tcase \"unknown\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeUnknown\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeUnknown\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// MustParseErrorType creates an ErrorType from a string.\n// Panics if the string doesn't match any known type.\n//\n// This function is intended for use in initialization code where error types\n// are expected to be constant and correct.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrorType := MustParseErrorType(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: ErrTypeRequired\n//\n//\t// This will panic:\n//\terrorType := MustParseErrorType(\"invalid_type\")\nfunc MustParseErrorType(s string) ErrorType {\n\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(s)\n\tif et == ErrTypeUnknown && s != \"unknown\" {\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid error type: %s\", s))\n\t}\n\treturn et\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE COLLECTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorTypeList represents a collection of ErrorType values.\ntype ErrorTypeList []ErrorType\n\n// AllErrorTypes contains all defined error types.\nvar AllErrorTypes = ErrorTypeList{\n\tErrTypeRequired,\n\tErrTypeFormat,\n\tErrTypeRange,\n\tErrTypeLength,\n\tErrTypeType,\n\tErrTypeValue,\n\tErrTypeDuplicate,\n\tErrTypeConflict,\n\tErrTypeUnknown,\n}\n\n// StructuralErrorTypes contains error types related to data structure validation.\nvar StructuralErrorTypes = ErrorTypeList{\n\tErrTypeRequired,\n\tErrTypeType,\n\tErrTypeLength,\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n130\t\n131\t// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n132\t//\n133\t// Parameters:\n134\t//   - expected: The expected status code (e.g., 200) or codes (e.g., []int{200, 201})\n135\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n136\t//   - context: Optional context FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n193\t//\n194\t// Parameters:\n195\t//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n196\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n197\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n198\t//\n199\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\n200\t//\n201\t// Example usage:\n202\t//\n203\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\")\n204\t//\t// Output:\n205\t//\t// status_code_range parameters for errors\n211\t//\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n212\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n213\tfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n214\t\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\n215\t\n216\t\t// Build range info string\n217\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", min, max)\n218\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n219\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", min, max, desc)\n220\t\t}\n221\t\n222\t\tdetails := []string{}\n223\t\tif err != nil {\n224\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern error: %v\", err))\n225\t\t} else {\n226\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside range %d-%d\", actual, min, max))\n227\t\t}\n228\t\n229\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n230\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", pattern, header mismatches.\n244\t//\n245\t// Parameters:\n246\t//   - expected: The expected content type (e.g., \"application/json\")\n247\t//   - actual: The actual content type received\n248\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n249\t//\n250\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n251\t//\n252\t// Example usage:\n253\t//\n254\t//\terr :=     - Check if charset or boundary parameters are needed\n263\t//\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\n264\tfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n273\t//\n274\t// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n275\t// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n276\t// your specific requirements.\n277\t//\n278\t// Parameters:\n279\t//   - validationType: The category of validation\n280\t//   - expected: The expected value\n281\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n282\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\n283\t//\n284\t// Example usage:\n285\t//\n286\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n287\t//\t    \"custom_field\",\n288\t//\t    \"required_value\",\n289\t//\t    \"actual_value\",\n290\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n291\t//\t    validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n292\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n293\t//\t)\n294\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n295\t\tvalidationType string,\n296\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n297\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n298\t) ValidationError {\n299\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n300\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n301\t\t\topt(config)\n302\t\t}\n303\t\n304\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n305\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n306\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n307\t\n308\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n309\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n310\t\t}\n311\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n312\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n313\t\t}\n314\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n315\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n316\t\t}\n317\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n318\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n319\t\t}\n320\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n321\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n322\t\t}\n323\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n324\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n325\t\t}\n326\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n327\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n328\t\t}\n329\t\n330\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\t// =============================================================================\n334\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n335\t// =============================================================================\n336\t\n337\t// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\n338\ttype FormatConfig struct {\n339\t\tContext            string\n340\t\tResponseSnippet    string\n341\t\tFieldName          string\n342\t\tPatternDetails     string\n343\t\tRangeInfo          string\n344\t\tValidationDetails  []string\n345\t\tSuggestions        []string\n346\t\t// Category-aware and severity support\n347\t\tSeverityOverride   ErrorSeverity\n348\t\tCategoryHint       ErrorCategory\n349\t\tStatusCode         int\n350\t\tTimeout            int\n351\t\tSecurityContext    string\n352\t}\n353\t\n354\t// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\n355\ttype FormatOption {\n360\t\t\tc.Context = context\n361\t\t}\n362\t}\n363\t\n364\t// WithResponseSnippet creates a FormatOption that sets the response snippet.\n365\tfunc WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption {\n366\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n367\t\t\tc.ResponseSnippet = snippet\n368\t\t}\n369\t}\n370\t\n371\t// WithFieldName creates a FormatOption that sets the field name.\n372\tfunc WithFieldName(fieldName string) FormatOption {\n373\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n374\t\t\tc.FieldName = fieldName\n375\t\t}\n376\t}\n377\t\n378\t// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that WithRangeInfo creates a FormatOption that sets range information.\n386\tfunc WithRangeInfo(info string) FormatOption {\n387\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n388\t\t\tc.RangeInfo = info\n389\t\t}\n390\t}\n391\t\n392\t// WithValidationDetails creates a FormatOption that adds validation details.\n393\tfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption {\n394\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n395\t\t\tc.ValidationDetails = append(c.ValidationDetails, details...)\n396\t\t}\n397\t}\n398\t\n399\t// WithSuggestions creates a FormatOption that sets custom
